name: Breedon Group plc
executive_summary:
# Breedon Group plc (BREE.L) — First-Pass Research Memo

**Sector:** Basic Materials (construction materials)  
**Quantitative screen:** Buy (8/22 models, composite 59%)  
**Source note:** No annual or interim filing bodies are available in the research library; financial analysis below falls back to Yahoo (`financials_annual.json`) throughout.

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Breedon Group plc screens as a buy on a multi-factor value basis, led by cheapness (P/B 0.9, EV/EBITDA 6.1), a 10.2% FCF yield, and a 4.8% dividend yield passing eight of 22 quantitative models across cheapness, dividend, GARP, and risk families. The investment case rests on a UK–Ireland construction-materials platform with growing US exposure, trading below book and generating solid free cash flow despite a cyclical UK downturn. The central debate is whether revenue and EBITDA resilience—plus M&A-led growth (notably the ~$120m Falling Springs quarry acquisition)—will offset declining reported operating profit and net income, alongside a material step-up in net debt to £481m. Primary RNS annual and interim results are absent from the filing index with no downloadable bodies, limiting verification of pensions, covenants, and going-concern language; deep research therefore partially confirms the screen on valuation but flags cyclical and leverage risks.

---

## INVESTMENT THESIS

For a value investor, Breedon offers a classic “quality cyclical at a discount” profile. The quantitative screen passes Deep Value, FCF Yield, Neff PEGY, High Dividend Yield, Dividend Growth, Magic Formula, Dreman Contrarian, and Financial Health—covering all four signal families (cheapness, dividend, GARP, risk) with high data quality (19/20 metrics). At P/E 12.9 and P/B 0.9, the market prices in sustained UK construction weakness and integration risk from an acquisitive strategy, yet underlying cash generation remains robust: Yahoo-sourced FCF rose to £105.8m in FY2025 from £70.4m in FY2024, supporting a dividend that grew to £51.1m paid (from £48.1m) while the company extended its share buyback programme (per RNS headlines in the filing index).

Business quality is anchored in vertically integrated aggregates, cement, asphalt, and ready-mixed concrete operations with local quarry moats and logistics advantages—assets reflected in £1.04bn net PPE and a growing US footprint. Revenue grew to £1,713.8m in 2025 (+8.7% YoY per Yahoo), and management described growth in reported revenue and underlying EBITDA in 2025 despite a “testing year” of deferred infrastructure projects and sharp UK materials demand weakness (Breedon Group H2 Earnings Call Highlights, 11 Mar 2026). The screen’s 7.1% ROE is modest and reflects cyclical margin compression (operating income fell to £131.3m from £155.9m), but the combination of sub-book valuation, double-digit FCF yield, and income characteristics aligns well with a patient value accumulation strategy rather than a growth-at-any-price mandate.

---

## FINANCIAL REVIEW

**Filing coverage gap:** `filings_index.json` (UK RNS regime) contains 20 announcements, all classified as `other` (director dealings, share issues, buybacks, acquisition notice). There are **zero** `annual` or `interim` entries and **zero** body extracts under `filings/bodies/`. FY2025 full-year and H1 2025/H2 trading figures cannot be verified from primary filings in this library. All figures below are from **Yahoo `financials_annual.json` (fallback)**.

### Income and profitability trend (Yahoo, FY year-ends)

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | FY2023 | FY2022 |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Revenue (£m) | 1,713.8 | 1,576.3 | 1,487.5 | 1,396.3 |
| Operating income (£m) | 131.3 | 155.9 | 146.2 | 147.6 |
| EBITDA (£m) | 272.7 | 262.1 | 241.9 | 235.4 |
| Net income (£m) | 83.8 | 96.2 | 105.5 | 112.5 |
| Diluted EPS (p) | 24.2 | 28.0 | 31.0 | 33.2 |

Revenue has compounded upward (+23% over four years), driven by acquisitions and pricing/mix, but reported earnings have trended down from a 2022 peak—EPS has fallen roughly 27% over three years. FY2025 shows revenue growth alongside margin pressure: operating income declined 16% despite EBITDA rising 4% to £272.7m, suggesting depreciation/amortisation and cost inflation (D&A rose to £138.5m from £112.2m). Restructuring and M&A-related charges (£5.4m in 2025 per Yahoo) remain a recurring drag.

Interest expense increased to £28.9m (from £24.5m), consistent with higher leverage after debt-funded acquisitions.

### Balance sheet and leverage (Yahoo)

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | FY2023 |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Total debt (£m) | 642.8 | 475.3 | 296.8 |
| Net debt (£m) | 481.1 | 356.6 | 121.9 |
| Equity (£m) | 1,196.7 | 1,170.2 | 1,110.4 |
| Goodwill + intangibles (£m) | 792.1 | 686.3 | 520.2 |
| Tangible book (£m) | 404.6 | 483.9 | 590.2 |

Net debt rose £124.5m in FY2025 alone. Implied net debt/EBITDA (Yahoo) is ~1.8x (£481.1m / £272.7m)— manageable for a quarry operator but materially higher than the ~0.5x level in 2023. Goodwill of £564m against tangible book of £405m signals acquisition-heavy growth and impairment sensitivity if US/UK markets weaken further. Long-term provisions of £88.7m (restoration, pensions, etc.) are present on the balance sheet but cannot be dissected without annual report notes.

### Cash flow and capital allocation (Yahoo)

| Metric | FY2025 | FY2024 | FY2023 |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Operating cash flow (£m) | 225.9 | 201.7 | 191.9 |
| Capex (£m) | (120.1) | (131.3) | (106.8) |
| Free cash flow (£m) | 105.8 | 70.4 | 85.1 |
| Acquisitions (£m) | (159.9) | (173.6) | (18.8) |
| Dividends paid (£m) | (51.1) | (48.1) | (37.6) |
| Net debt issuance (£m) | +143.9 | +53.4 | (0.9) |

FCF improved sharply in 2025 despite lower net income, aided by working-capital release and lower capex. However, acquisition spend of £159.9m plus dividends of £51.1m exceeded FCF, with the gap funded by £166m long-term debt issuance—an aggressive capital-allocation posture that supports growth but elevates financial risk. The screen’s FCF figure of ~£110m and 10.2% yield align with this Yahoo FCF trajectory.

### Interim results

No interim (half-year) filings are indexed. H2 2025 commentary in news (earnings call summary, 11 Mar 2026) indicates management reported revenue and underlying EBITDA growth for full-year 2025 in a weak UK market, but precise H1/H2 splits, covenant headroom, and pension deficit figures are **unavailable** in the source set.

---

## RISKS AND RED FLAGS

**Cyclical UK construction exposure.** News coverage consistently flags UK construction gloom (Investors’ Chronicle, “Breedon struggles to escape UK construction gloom,” 11 Mar 2026). Management itself cited deferred infrastructure projects and a sharp downturn in materials demand. A prolonged UK slowdown could compress volumes and pricing faster than cost actions offset.

**Leverage and M&A integration.** Net debt has quadrupled since 2023 on the back of serial acquisitions (£160m+ annually in 2024–25 per Yahoo). The June 2026 Falling Springs quarry deal (~$120m, TipRanks, 8 Jun 2026) extends US exposure but adds integration and execution risk. Without filing bodies, covenant terms, maturity profile, and going-concern assessments **cannot be reviewed**—a material research gap.

**Earnings quality and goodwill.** Reported operating profit and net income are declining while revenue rises, implying margin erosion or acquisition-related costs. Goodwill/intangibles (£792m) exceed tangible equity (£405m); impairment risk rises if quarry cash flows disappoint.

**Rising financing costs.** Interest expense trended up to £28.9m; further rate exposure or refinancing at higher spreads would pressure EPS and dividend cover (not calculable precisely without interim dividend per-share data from filings).

**Governance / capital structure noise.** The filing index shows multiple director/PDMR dealings, share issues (Feb 2026), and buyback extensions—none inherently negative, but equity issuance alongside debt-funded M&A warrants monitoring for dilution.

**Pension and environmental liabilities.** Long-term provisions (£88.7m) and deferred tax liabilities (£102.9m) are on the balance sheet; restoration obligations are standard for quarry operators. Specific pension deficit or environmental contingency language is **not available** without annual report extracts.

**Data and coverage limitations.** Absence of primary annual/interim filing bodies means key risk disclosures (related-party transactions, litigation, tax disputes) cannot be assessed in this pass.

---

## NEWS HIGHLIGHTS

Coverage over the past year is moderate, skewed toward earnings commentary, analyst target revisions, and M&A rather than deep investigative reporting. Several “Home and Away” soap headlines are irrelevant noise.

**Strategy and M&A**
- “Breedon boosts US footprint with $120m Falling Springs quarry deal” (TipRanks, 8 Jun 2026) — material US expansion via quarry acquisition; filing index also lists an “Acquisition” RNS (9 Jun 2026), body unavailable.
- “Breedon awaits brighter days” (Investors’ Chronicle, 23 Jul 2025) — earlier framing of cyclical recovery expectations.

**Results and operating environment**
- “Breedon Group H2 Earnings Call Highlights” (Yahoo Finance, 11 Mar 2026) — management reported FY2025 revenue and underlying EBITDA growth despite a “testing year”; highlighted UK demand weakness and deferred infrastructure work.
- “Breedon struggles to escape UK construction gloom” (Investors’ Chronicle, 11 Mar 2026) — sceptical take on cyclical headwinds.

**Capital returns and corporate actions**
- “Transaction in own shares and Buy Back Extension” (RNS headline, 17 Jul 2026) — ongoing buyback programme.
- “Issue of Shares” (Investegate, 11 Feb 2026) — equity issuance, likely linked to acquisition financing or employee schemes.
- Dividend-focused articles: “Be Sure To Check Out Breedon Group plc (LON:BREE) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend” (Yahoo Finance, 24 May 2026).

**Analyst sentiment**
- Multiple articles (Mar–Jun 2026) note analyst fair-value and price-target trims (e.g. resets toward £4.05–£4.50 from higher prior levels), reflecting cautious rather than bearish Street positioning.
- “Breedon Group PLC (BREE.L) Stock Analysis: 39% Potential Upside and Strong Buy Ratings” (DirectorsTalk Interviews, 11 Mar 2026) — promotional bullish counterpoint.

**Insider activity**
- Breedon featured in “Top 3 Undervalued European Small Caps With Insider Action” (Yahoo Finance, 27 Jan 2026) and similar listicles—suggestive but not substantiated with transaction detail in the manifest.

News coverage is **adequate for strategy and cycle colour** but **thin on regulatory, pension, or governance detail**; no material regulatory actions or management changes are flagged in the manifest.

---

## RESEARCH VERDICT

Verdict: accumulate  
Risk: medium  
Confidence: 0.68  
Rationale: Valuation and cash-flow metrics strongly support the quantitative buy signal, but missing primary filings, rising leverage, and cyclical UK margin pressure prevent full confirmation of balance-sheet and covenant safety.

---

*Sources: `screening_snapshot.json`, `financials_annual.json` (Yahoo fallback), `filings_index.json`, `news_manifest.json`. Macro context unavailable (`macro_context.json` — snapshot missing).*
